content.problemStatement:
No, we won't be talking the next day 

I ain't got nothin' to say (Hell nah) 

I ain't lookin' for my one true love 

Yeah, that ship sailed away

Shivansh and Tina are playing a game. The players move in alternating turns with Shivansh starting first.

They have integers NNN and KKK. In one move, a player has to:


Choose any non-negative integer XXX such that KX≤NK^X \le NKX≤N;
Subtract KXK^XKX from NNN.
The player who cannot make a move loses. If both players play optimally, find the winner of the game.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK — as mentioned in the statement.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line `Shivansh` if Shivansh wins the game, otherwise `Tina`.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings `Tina`, `TINA`, `tina`, and `TiNa` will all be treated as identical).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111:

Shivansh chooses X=1X = 1X=1 and subtracts 212^{1}21 from 222 to get 000.

Since Tina cannot make a move now, Shivansh wins.
Test case 222:

Shivansh chooses X=1X = 1X=1 and subtracts 313^{1}31 from 444 to get 111.
Tina chooses X=0X = 0X=0 and subtracts 303^{0}30 from 111 to get 000.

Since Shivansh cannot make a move now, Tina wins.
Test case 333: Shivansh cannot reduce the number further, it is already 000, so he loses.
